untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options strangle strategy (simultaneous long call and long put at different strikes) with specific premium costs and expiration months. The backtest engine uses price-based indicators only. Options-specific parameters (strike prices, premiums, expiration dates, intrinsic value at expiration) cannot be translated into price/technical indicator conditions without a dedicated options pricing model. The signal requires: (1) options contract selection logic, (2) premium payment tracking, (3) intrinsic value calculation at expiration, (4) multi-leg position management — none of which are supported by the allowed indicators list.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_127 is an options strategy, not a price-action or technical indicator-based trade. A proper implementation would require: options chain data, strike selection rules, implied volatility inputs, and profit/loss calculation at expiration based on underlying price relative to two strikes (50 call, 60 put). The 10-point spread (60-50) represents maximum profit, achieved only if price closes outside both strikes at October expiration. This cannot be meaningfully backested using only OHLCV price indicators.
